trends.name: Decentralized Agentic Mesh Protocols

technical_indicators.github_metrics: Significant star growth in 'Agent-to-Agent' (A2A) communication libraries; emergence of Rust-based agent runtimes.
technical_indicators.developer_interest: Shift from prompt-engineering to multi-agent state persistence and recursive task decomposition.
technical_indicators.key_tech: WASM-based agent sandboxing, p2p discovery protocols.

trends.narrative_summary: The transition from single-purpose LLM wrappers to persistent, autonomous meshes that negotiate resources and tasks without human intervention.

trends.name: Sub-1-bit LLM Architectures

technical_indicators.github_metrics: Rapid adoption of BitNet-derivative repositories; 300% increase in forks for ternary-weight optimized kernels.
technical_indicators.developer_interest: High focus on running 100B+ parameter models on consumer-grade mobile hardware and edge devices.
technical_indicators.key_tech: 1.58-bit quantization, custom Triton kernels for bit-manipulation.

trends.narrative_summary: Open-source momentum is pivoting away from 'dense' FP16/BF16 models toward native bit-linear architectures, democratizing the deployment of large-scale reasoning models.

trends.name: SMR-to-Compute Grid Integration

technical_indicators.github_metrics: Increased activity in open-source thermal management and digital twin frameworks for Gen-IV reactors.
technical_indicators.developer_interest: Systems engineers focusing on 'Behind-the-Meter' (BTM) nuclear power for AI hyperscalers.
technical_indicators.key_tech: Molten salt coolant monitoring, AI-driven predictive maintenance for micro-reactors.

trends.narrative_summary: The physical convergence of nuclear energy and AI infrastructure, where Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) are being directly integrated into modular data center designs to solve the US power grid bottleneck.

trends.name: GPT-5 Anticipation & Impact
trends.summary: There's growing anticipation surrounding the potential release of GPT-5 and its implications. Discussions center on whether it will live up to the hype and its potential impact on the field.
evidence: Aug 8, 2025: Discussion about the potential release of GPT-5 and its capabilities.

trends.name: Open Source LLM Infrastructure & Deployment
trends.summary: Increased discussion around open-source LLMs, specifically relating to deployment and infrastructure challenges.  Focus on machine availability and potential issues with long-term access.
evidence: open source thinking is open, publishing, and usable.
Regular machine purchases are showing as open source.
Mar 19, 2026: Mentions API availability and potential future releases, indicating ongoing development and deployment efforts.

trends.name: Dzip Compression Technology
trends.summary: A new compression technology, 'dzip', is gaining traction, potentially offering improvements over traditional zip formats.  Early reports suggest faster compression/decompression speeds.
evidence: Apr 7, 2023: Mentions dzip compression and its potential usability, suggesting a new compression method.
